name: GHGSat

overview.value: GHGSat Inc. operates a satellite constellation for monitoring targeted greenhouse gas emitters. Its GHGSat D satellite demonstrates a miniature hyperspectral SWIR imaging spectrometer designed to detect emissions from sources such as tailing ponds, landfills, and industrial stacks including flaring and venting.

base.reason: trade press (Waste Dive, Dec 2, 2025), states 'The company now has 16 satellites orbiting the earth and taking measurement of industrial facilities for greenhouse gas emission leaks'; single source, no corroboration crawl run this maintenance pass
trade press (Waste Dive, Dec 2, 2025); the 16-satellite figure is stated as satellites 'orbiting the earth and taking measurement', read as an active count

established aggregator; page documents GHGSat-C16 (Eleonore) and GHGSat-C17 (Nuray) launched 2026-07-07 aboard SpaceX Transporter-17 from Vandenberg SLC-4E, corroborated by press search (satellitetoday.com Transporter-17 coverage)

eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states the primary sensor 'has a spatial resolution of approximately 30 m with a swath width of less than 15 km'; registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms

eoPortal mission page; page states the instrument observes a 'spectral range from 1600 - 1700 nm with multiple bands in a proprietary configuration', selected for the spectral features of the target gases

notes: eoPortal EO spec backfill (2026-07-09): greenhouse-gas (methane/CO2) monitoring microsatellite constellation; primary sensor spatial resolution approximately 30 m with a swath width of less than 15 km (bound, so swath_km left null); imaging spectrometer observes 1600-1700 nm. Revisit not stated on the page, left null. Page last updated Jan 24, 2026. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-13): the fr.wikipedia 14/12 figures were stale. Gunter's Space Page (two catalog pages, ghgsat-d.htm and ghgsat-c9.htm) individually documents 18 named satellites through GHGSat-C17 (Eleonore/C16 and Nuray/C17 launched 2026-07-07 on Transporter-17), but per the no-summing-across-pages rule that count was not written as a field value. Instead sats_launched_total/sats_active_claimed were set to 16, the last figure explicitly stated by a single source (Waste Dive, Dec 2, 2025, pre-dating the C16/C17 launch). This likely undercounts the true current total by 2; flagged for Florian rather than estimated. latest_launch_date was updated to the Gunter's-documented 2026-07-07 launch since that is a single-page dated fact, not a summed total.

events.headline: GHGSat launches GHGSat-D (Claire), its first greenhouse gas monitoring satellite
GHGSat-D demonstrates hyperspectral instrument for monitoring point-source gas emitters
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C1 (Iris), its first commercial operational satellite
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C2 (Hugo) aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C3 (Luca), expanding its monitoring constellation
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C4, C5 and C6 on SpaceX Transporter-7 rideshare
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C9, C10 and C11 as hosted payloads on SpaceX Transporter-9
GHGSat wins CAD 2 million Canadian Space Agency award for methane spectrometer
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C14 (Teodor) and GHGSat-C15 (Laila) on SpaceX Transporter-15
GHGSat launches GHGSat-C16 (Eleonore) and GHGSat-C17 (Nuray) on SpaceX Transporter-17
